q: What has a face and two hands but no arms or legs?
What does a house wear?
Where would you take a sick boat?
I give milk and I have a horn, but I'm not a cow. What am I?
Why did the fly never land on the computer?
a: He was afraid of the world wide web.

q: What creature is smarter than a talking parrot?
What rock group has four famous men, but none of them sing?
What kind of murderer is full of fiber?
I have hundreds of wheels, but I do not move. What am I?
How does a bee get to school?
What kind of running leads to walking?
I start with M, end with X, and can have any number of letters. What am I?
Why are A's like flowers?
What is orange and sounds like a parrot?
I make a loud sound when I change. When I do, I get bigger but weigh less. What am I?
Why did the skeleton skip the party?
a: He had no body to go with.

q: I travel all around the world but never leave the corner. What am I?
What can go up a chimney down, but can't go down a chimney up?
How do you spell COW in thirteen letters?
What has wheels and flies, but is not an aircraft?
What do you call a bear without ears?
What do you get if you cross poison ivy with a four-leaf clover?
Why is Europe like a frying pan?
a: It has Greece at the bottom.

q: What do you call a fly without wings?
What word has five letters but sounds like it only has one?
What is 3/7 chicken, 2/3 cat, and 2/4 goat?
Which part of the street do ghosts live at?
What do Alexander the Great and Winnie the Pooh have in common?
What type of music do rabbits listen to?
During what month do people sleep the least?
What is on the ground and also a hundred feet in the air?
I have a neck but no head, and I wear a cap. What am I?
What kind of room has no doors or windows?
What does it take to make an octopus laugh?
You cannot keep me until you have given me. What am I?
What is easy to get into but hard to get out of?
What begins with T, ends with T, and has T in it?
What is full of holes but still holds water?
What is always in front of you but can't be seen?
How do you make the number one disappear by adding to it?
a: Add the letter G — it becomes Gone.

q: I'm tall when I'm young, I'm short when I'm old. What am I?
Which word becomes shorter when you add 2 letters to it?
What's worth more after it's broken?
What do geometry teachers have decorating their floor?
What begins with an E but only has one letter in it?
What belongs to you but is used most often by everyone else?
If you throw a blue stone into the Red Sea, what will it become?
What goes up and down the stairs without moving?
What word is spelled wrong in every dictionary?
I shrink smaller every time I take a bath. What am I?
How many bananas can you eat if your stomach is empty?
a: Just one — after that it's not empty anymore.

q: What falls often but never gets hurt?
How far can you walk into a forest?
a: One step — then you're walking through the forest.

q: Which two keys cannot open any doors?
Which band never plays music?
Why don't lobsters share?
Why was trigonometry math class so long?
a: The teacher kept going off on a tangent.

q: What cruel person would sit on a baby?
What is red and smells like paint?
